items.title: Police got tip about 'dirty cop' helping trafficker 15 years before Winnipeg officer's arrest: warrant docs
items.summary_auto: One of the first red flags about now disgraced Winnipeg officer Elston Bostock came 15 years before he was arrested, when an informant warned police about a "dirty cop" helping a drug trafficker, search warrant documents obtained by CBC News say.

items.title: Loblaw says 'some stores' saw payment systems outage Friday, but issue now resolved
items.summary_auto: Customers at Loblaw-connected stores in multiple provinces were advised of interruptions to credit and debit systems on Friday afternoon, though the company says the reported issues have since been resolved.

items.title: 3 options to replace 'inadequate' prime minister housing
items.summary_auto: An internal memo obtained by CBC News reveals that Rideau Cottage, the residence for past and the current prime minister, is inadequate and comes with security risks, according to the government’s own assessment. The two-storey brick house was meant to be a temporary home for leaders, after 24 Sussex — the official residence — was decommissioned in 2022.

items.title: Stock markets dip for another straight week as U.S. war on Iran continues
items.summary_auto: Canadian and U.S. stock markets sank on Friday amid fears about the effects of the U.S.-Iran war on interest rates.

items.title: Friend of Sikh student killed on Alberta highway recalls deadly daytime shooting
items.summary_auto: Birinder Singh was driving down the highway, hoping to see Alberta’s Rocky Mountains for the first time, when a bullet from a passing vehicle ended his life.

items.title: No, we don't need high-protein boxed mac and cheese, experts say. But people want it
items.summary_auto: Kraft Heinz has just announced it's launching a high-protein mac and cheese called PowerMac that delivers 17 grams of protein and six grams of fibre per serving. But did we ... need this?

items.title: 2 men accused of fathering hundreds of kids banned from donating sperm in Quebec
items.summary_auto: Quebec's Superior Court has temporarily barred a man and his son from donating sperm after they were accused of fathering hundreds of children.

items.title: Alberta to close supervised consumption sites in Calgary, Lethbridge at end of June
items.summary_auto: Nearly five years after first announcing its plans to close the supervised consumption site at the Sheldon M. Chumir Health Centre, the Alberta government set a date to close the site later this year and replace it with services more focused on recovery and treatment.

items.title: Alberta’s 'Peterson law' leads lawyers' regulator to stop mandating Indigenous education course
items.summary_auto: The regulator for Alberta’s lawyers says it will no longer mandate Indigenous cultural competency training in advance of what Alberta Premier Danielle Smith calls the “Peterson law” coming into effect.

items.title: As Middle Eastern countries struggle to down Iranian drones, Ukraine sends 200 specialists to help
items.summary_auto: After Iranian Shahed drones hit an oil refinery in Kuwait, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirmed that Kyiv has sent more than 200 military specialists to the Middle East to help defend critical infrastructure from the same type of threat Ukraine has faced for years.

items.title: Netflix, Warner Music sign multi-year deal to produce artist documentaries
items.summary_auto: Netflix ​and Warner Music Group have signed an exclusive multi-year deal to produce documentary series and films exploring the lives, music and legacies of the label's storied artist ‌roster.

items.title: Why Alberta’s separation from Canada is almost impossible | About That
items.summary_auto: Alberta's separatist sentiment has resurged in recent months amid the Trump administration's comments about the province's future, coupled with economic and political tensions with the Canadian government. Andrew Chang explains what it would actually take to grant sovereignty to a Canadian province, and why it's so difficult to achieve. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Celebrating Eid al-Fitr
items.summary_auto: Worshippers are celebrating Eid al-Fitr, the three-day festival that marks the end of Ramadan, the holy month of fasting.

items.title: Palestinian man says Israeli settlers sexually assaulted him in front of his family
items.summary_auto: Israeli settlers sexually assaulted a Palestinian man — tying his genitalia with zip ties and parading him naked in front of his family — during a mid-March attack in the occupied West Bank, according to the victim and eyewitnesses.

items.title: Refugee family faces separation as father and son ordered deported from Canada
BTS to stage long-awaited comeback show at Seoul landmark
items.summary_auto: BTS will stage its long-awaited comeback concert on Saturday night at Seoul's Gwanghwamun Square, one of South Korea's most famous landmarks that represents its royal heritage and political and cultural life.

items.title: Chuck Norris, Hollywood martial artist and actor, dead at 86
items.summary_auto: Martial arts phenom and Hollywood action star Chuck Norris — known for Walker, Texas Ranger, among other macho roles — died on Thursday at the age of 86, in what his family described as a "sudden passing."

items.title: Ontario proposing to cap ticket resale prices at original value
items.summary_auto: The Ontario government is planning to outlaw sky-high ticket resale prices, according to a news release. The move comes years after the province scrapped part of an anti-scalping law in 2019.

items.title: Couple airlifted from home as heavy rain triggers mudslide in B.C.’s Fraser Valley
items.summary_auto: Heavy rains battering B.C. caused a mudslide in Coquitlam Thursday, forcing one couple to be airlifted out, and triggered two evacuation alerts in the Fraser Valley, along the Chilliwack River.

items.title: These Canadians are helping white supremacists profit from hateful livestreams
Iran women's soccer team greeted with a welcome ceremony in Tehran
items.summary_auto: The Iranian women's soccer team crossed the Turkish border into Iran on Wednesday to complete a fraught return journey from Australia, after five members withdrew asylum claims they had lodged ‌there.

items.title: B.C. premier faces pressure over proposed changes to landmark reconciliation framework law
items.summary_auto: The NDP government is meeting with First Nations groups over the changes it plans to make to the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act, or DRIPA. Premier David Eby is facing pressure from Indigenous leaders to leave the law alone, and from Conservative politicians to scrap it entirely.

items.title: Canadian company helping white supremacists fundraise from hateful livestreams
items.summary_auto: A Canadian streaming platform is helping white supremacists who have been kicked off mainstream social media continue to profit from hateful content, an investigation by CBC’s the fifth estate has found.

items.title: Customers complain about long wait times, multiple calls to resolve issues with Rogers, Telus and Bell
items.summary_auto: More than a dozen customers told Marketplace they are frustrated with poor customer service they received from Canada’s big three telecoms. Employees at Rogers and Telus said frontline customer service representatives have less incentive to help customers issue credits or lower bills, and said they’re measured on their abilities to increase customers’ bills.

items.title: Ryan Gosling's Project Hail Mary is the 1st banger of the year
items.summary_auto: The Ryan Gosling-led Project Hail Mary does, in many ways, feel like a lowercase Interstellar: a fantastically good time at the movies, if not quite as insightful or far-reaching as a generational classic. But it's still everything you could want from a movie — it's smart and it's fun.
